overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on topics related to computational social science, disinformation, and research opportunities. He has a substantial following across all platforms, with LinkedIn having the largest number of followers. Engagement varies by platform, with LinkedIn posts receiving more comments than other platforms.

contentThemes.description: Posts announcing new research findings, publications, or grant awards.
Content related to the field of computational social science, including methods and applications.
Posts discussing disinformation, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and related topics.
Posts advertising job openings, postdoctoral positions, or research opportunities for students.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to reach a wider audience.
Use platform-specific hashtags to increase visibility.
Engage with comments and replies to foster a sense of community.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., images, videos) to see what resonates best with each audience.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows strong engagement through reposts, suggesting an active community willing to share content. Focusing on timely research publications and posing questions could further improve engagement.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is primarily driven by likes and reposts. Focus on sharing thought-provoking discussions and updates on tools/algorithms related to detecting coordinated behavior.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn engagement is high with likes and comments, indicating a professional audience. Sharing career opportunities and research grant announcements performs well.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
